profile.tagline: Zero-knowledge audit trails for BNB Smart Chain — prove compliance without exposing business logic.
profile.full_description: ZKBL lets BNB Smart Chain protocols satisfy institutional audit requirements by replacing raw privileged-event logs with Groth16 ZK proof receipts stored in an on-chain registry contract. When a BEP-20 mint, ownership transfer, or BEP-177 governance execution fires, ZKBL's Node.js proof server compiles the event against user-defined YAML policy rules — multisig thresholds, timelock durations, caller allowlists — and posts a verifiable compliance receipt to BSC instead of the raw calldata. Auditors query the public registry via RPC to confirm policy adherence without reading transaction arguments, fee structures, or wallet identities. A Hardhat and Foundry SDK plugin reduces integration to a single config line in existing deploy pipelines, targeting DeFi vaults, launchpads, and RWA platforms closing deals with TradFi counterparties.
tech_stack: IPFS / Pinata (circuit artifact and proving key pinning)
profile.team_description: The founding team combines a smart contract security engineer with prior audit experience at a BSC-focused firm and a cryptography engineer who has shipped Circom circuits for production ZK applications. Together they have hands-on experience across Solidity security, ZK proof system integration, and developer tooling for EVM-compatible chains.

roadmap.goal: Ship a functional MVP covering core Groth16 circuit compilation and on-chain registry for BEP-20 privileged events.
deliverables: YAML-to-Circom compiler supporting multisig threshold, timelock, and allowlist policy rules with end-to-end Groth16 proof generation via SnarkJS
Solidity 0.8.x registry contract deployed on BSC testnet that accepts and stores proof receipts bound to BNB block hash and per-event nonce
Hardhat SDK plugin (npm package) enabling one-line instrumentation of existing deploy scripts to emit compliance receipts alongside BEP-20 mint and ownership transfer transactions

roadmap.goal: Extend coverage to BEP-177 governance events, ship the Foundry plugin, and launch the read-only auditor portal on BSC mainnet.
deliverables: Circom circuit templates for BEP-177 governance execution events including proposal ID binding and quorum threshold verification
Foundry forge scripting plugin published to the Foundry registry with parity feature coverage to the Hardhat plugin

Read-only auditor web portal (Next.js, wagmi) allowing institutional auditors to query the BSC mainnet registry by contract address and verify proof receipts against live on-chain state without wallet connection

roadmap.goal: Onboard three paying protocol teams to mainnet, harden proof server reliability, and add IPFS-pinned circuit artifact versioning for long-term audit reproducibility.
deliverables: Production proof generation server with job queue (BullMQ + Redis), retry logic, and sub-30-second end-to-end proof latency benchmarked on BEP-20 mint circuits
IPFS circuit artifact registry storing versioned proving keys and WASM binaries via Pinata, with on-chain content-hash anchoring so auditors can reproduce any historical proof independently

Three signed mainnet integrations with BNB Chain protocol teams (at least one RWA platform) generating live compliance receipts, with case-study documentation published for TradFi partner due diligence use

original_idea.tagline: Zero-knowledge audit trails for BNB Smart Chain contracts — prove compliance without exposing logic.
original_idea.problem: BNB Smart Chain protocols in 2026 face mounting regulatory pressure to demonstrate internal access controls, admin key usage, and privileged function call histories to auditors and institutional partners. Exposing raw on-chain logs reveals proprietary business logic, competitor-readable fee structures, and user-identifiable data. There is no primitive on BNB Chain that lets a protocol prove 'this admin action was authorized and policy-compliant' without revealing the underlying operational details.
original_idea.solution: ZKBL intercepts privileged on-chain events — BEP-20 mints, ownership transfers, pausable triggers, BEP-177 governance executions — and commits ZK proofs of policy compliance to a BNB Chain registry contract instead of raw calldata. Using Groth16 circuits compiled against user-defined policy rules (e.g., 'multisig threshold ≥ 3', 'cooldown period enforced', 'caller in allowlist'), ZKBL generates a verifiable compliance receipt that auditors can check without reading the underlying transaction arguments. A lightweight SDK wraps existing Hardhat and Foundry deploy pipelines so teams integrate in under a day.
original_idea.target_user: BNB Chain protocol teams — DeFi vaults, launchpads, and RWA platforms — that need to satisfy institutional or regulatory audit requirements without open-sourcing their operational security model or exposing user data to competitors.
original_idea.unique_insight: Compliance and privacy are treated as opposites, but ZK proofs make them composable — you can prove a rule was followed without proving anything else, which is exactly what auditors actually need.
core_features: Policy-as-code DSL: define compliance rules (multisig thresholds, timelock durations, caller allowlists) in a YAML config that compiles to Groth16 circuits
On-chain compliance registry: BNB Smart Chain contract that stores ZK proof receipts per privileged event, queryable by auditors via a public RPC endpoint
SDK wrapper for Hardhat and Foundry: one-line integration that auto-instruments existing deploy scripts to emit proofs alongside transactions
Audit portal: read-only dashboard where institutional auditors verify proof receipts against on-chain state without accessing raw calldata or business logic
Proof replay protection: each receipt binds to a BNB block hash and nonce, preventing replay attacks or retroactive compliance fabrication
original_idea.why_now: BNB Chain's push into RWA and institutional DeFi in 2025-2026 has brought a wave of protocols that need demonstrable compliance posture to close deals with TradFi partners — but existing audit tools (Tenderly, Etherscan) expose everything or nothing. The ZK tooling maturity curve (SnarkJS, Circom 2.x) now makes it feasible for a small team to ship production-grade circuits without a cryptography research department.
